programmes.show_description: Flavor World. Guy Fieri dives into cultural cuisine starting in Phoenix, Arizona. Then, he tries authentic Argentine in Boulder, Colorado, and Ethiopian meats in Memphis, Tennessee. S38, Ep15
Bird, Belly And Beef. Guy Fieri seeks out flavourful meat! He checks out Mexican pork sandwiches in Memphis, Tennessee, and rich short ribs in St. Simons Island, Georgia. S38, Ep16
Hearty Handfuls. Guy Fieri digs into hearty handfuls of major flavour, like next-level pizza in Tennessee, tasty tacos in Alberta, and elevated empanadas in Georgia. S38, Ep12
You Called It. Guy is on a mission to try out a variety of delicious treats. First, he heads to Kentucky to try a high-end chef's fresh French bread and then he heads to Minnesota. S9, Ep12
Bringin' It Home. Guy visits a family restaurant cooking Italian dishes in California. Then, in North Carolina, he goes to a neighbourhood favourite that serves delicious fish tacos. S9, Ep4
Homegrown And Homemade. In South Carolina, Guy Fieri visits a farmer's market. Plus, in Lackawanna, New York, he tries a lasagne loaded with stuffed peppers at an Italian restaurant. S9, Ep2
Neighborhood Favorites. Guy finds places regulars flock to. In Santa Fe, NM, a little cafe serves El Salvadoran specialties. In Mississippi a joint on stilts makes Creole dishes. S5, Ep7
Sauced And Spiced. Guy is looking for big flavour. In Tennessee he visits a place serving lamb with Syrian spiced tomato sauce. In Minnesota a café serves wild rice French toast. S10, Ep6

All Kinds Of Barbecue. Guy takes a southern swing to check out different styles of barbecue, including sweet ribs in Mississippi and tangy pulled pork in New Orleans. S3, Ep12
Neighborhood Favorites. Guy finds places regulars flock to. In Santa Fe, NM, a little cafe serves El Salvadoran specialties. In Mississippi a joint on stilts makes Creole dishes. S5, Ep7
Surf 'n' Turf Jackpot. Guy Fieri checks out surf and turf eateries from Mississippi to Hawaii. He visits a BBQ joint and a poke palace serving fish caught by the owner. S15, Ep4
Bringin' It Home. Guy visits a family restaurant cooking Italian dishes in California. Then, in North Carolina, he goes to a neighbourhood favourite that serves delicious fish tacos. S9, Ep4
Louisville. Adam visits Louisville to back 'Burrito Joe' as he races against the clock to consume 15 scoops of ice cream plus toppings in the Comfy Cow Challenge. S1, Ep10
Dallas. Adam goes to Dallas, Texas, to sample Texas-style bratwurst and BBQ brisket. And a Texan tries to devour five pounds of Vietnamese pho in under 30 minutes. S1, Ep12
Providence. Adam visits Providence, Rhode Island, for a unique grilled pizza. Later, he coaches a rugby player through 15 fully loaded hot wieners in 45 minutes. S1, Ep14
Route 66. Adam takes a road trip down Route 66, where palatable pit stops include steak and frozen custard. And in Tulsa, OK, a Navy officer aims to eat 23 puffy tacos in 90 minutes. S1, Ep16
Street Eats. Adam goes to Tampa, NYC and L.A. to sample the country's best street carts. He tries tacos and waffles before taking on a six-pound burrito with 'Big Dawg' in L.A. S1, Ep18
Milwaukee. Adam is in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, to sample the city's meats. And, a behemoth eater tries to devour a huge burger, fries and six blazing-hot wings in less than 23 minutes. S1, Ep11
Savannah. Adam is in Savannah, GA, to taste peach-glazed BBQ chicken and a shrimp and crab legs boil. And twin brothers have five minutes to eat a blazing-hot pulled pork sandwich. S1, Ep13
Portsmouth. Adam recruits Portsmouth local 'Chappy' Chapman to conquer 15 sliders, large fries and milkshake of the Slapshot Challenge, weighing in at over four pounds. S1, Ep15
Charleston. Adam Richman heads to Charleston, South Carolina for oysters served by the shovel, a fried chicken sandwich smothered in sausage gravy, and a spicy tuna sushi challenge. S2, Ep4
Knoxville. Adam Richman is in Knoxville, Tennessee, trying out some monumental hand-cut steaks, unique burgers and a five-pound barbecue burrito challenge. S2, Ep39
Devon. The next leg of James's trip sees him in Devon, joined by Michelin-starred chef, Simon Hulstone. They check out delicious local mushrooms and cheeses. S1, Ep3
Jersey. James heads to Jersey, where he's joined by pal and award-winning chef, Mark Jordan. They hit the road on mini motorbikes and learn to shuck oysters. S1, Ep4
Britain's best amateur bakers compete in the iconic white tent! Who will struggle under pressure and who will impress judges, Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith? S12, Ep1
Britain's best amateur bakers compete in the iconic white tent! Who will struggle under pressure and who will impress judges, Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith? S12, Ep2
Britain's best amateur bakers compete in the iconic white tent! Who will struggle under pressure and who will impress judges, Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith? S12, Ep3
Britain's best amateur bakers compete in the iconic white tent! Who will struggle under pressure and who will impress judges, Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith? S12, Ep4
Jazz. The bikers return to New Orleans, where Simon tries to convince Dave that jazz can be enjoyable. Plus, they cook classic local dishes, including jambalaya and gumbo. S1, Ep5
Country. The duo head to Nashville, Tennessee, to learn about the roots of country music. Plus, they cook biscuits and gravy and visit the Grand Ole Opry stage. S1, Ep6
Devon. The next leg of James's trip sees him in Devon, joined by Michelin-starred chef, Simon Hulstone. They check out delicious local mushrooms and cheeses. S1, Ep3

Newark, New Jersey. Casey Webb heads to his home state, New Jersey! He samples an iconic Italian hot dog, a Portuguese pork sandwich and takes part in a taco challenge. S5, Ep5
Brooklyn, New York. Casey Webb's quest takes him to Brooklyn for a barbeque and deli feast. Plus, he samples an Italian seafood staple and takes on a spicy shrimp challenge. S5, Ep6
Yorkshire Coast. The next leg of the Bikers' northern tour is along the Yorkshire coast. The duo begin in Scarborough to meet a seaweed farmer before heading to a vineyard in Aike. S1, Ep2
Quick And Easy: Game Food. Ree Drummond dishes up great ideas for quick and easy game-day meals. She knocks up a five-cheese pepperoni pizza and then a tasty millionaire shortbread. S23, Ep7
Ranchin' And Cookin'. Ree Drummond tackles a busy morning of ranching and get-ahead cooking. She creates the ultimate meatball and polenta casserole and hasselback chicken. S23, Ep8
Rome. In Rome, Jamie tracks down colourful, fresh and easy recipes. Plus, he is inspired to cook his own gnocchi with roasted tomato sauce and rocket pesto. S1, Ep7
Piedmont. Jamie travels to the snow-capped Italian Alps to seek out Piedmont's legendary comfort food. Plus, he cooks dumplings with roasted red onions and crispy pancetta. S1, Ep8
Fruit All Ways. Ina puts a new twist on fruit classics. She learns about desserts such as French nougatine from chef Laura Donnelly and makes wild rice salad and apple chutney. S7, Ep3
Italian At Home. Ina recreates a Florentine feast in East Hampton. There's a mozzarella master class, Italian drinks tray, shop-bought hors d'oeuvres and baked fish in parchment. S7, Ep4
French Week. It is the semi-final and there are just four bakers left, and the all-female semi-finalists have just three French bakes standing between them and a place in the final. S4, Ep9
Class Of 2012. Talking candidly about their experience, the Class of 2012 return to the Bake Off tent to give a special insight to life inside the competition. S4, Ep10
James indulges in the glitz and glamour of St. Tropez. He explores the local seafood market and, in true St. Tropez style, cooks his first dish aboard a yacht. S1, Ep11
James experiences the bustling Provencal port of Marseille. There he discovers local fishermen selling fresh seafood on the back of their boats. S1, Ep12

Taste Of The Sea. Marcus is on the hunt for the freshest catch on the island. He meets up with chef Bocho for some fresh local oysters and a beer on the beach. S1, Ep3
Tasty Tapas. Marcus explores Mallorcan tapas and tries clams, tortilla, and deep-fried courgette flower. He also discovers a local favourite, Vermouth. S1, Ep4
Rick visits a free-range goose farm in Leicester, inspiring him to tackle a roast goose dish. Plus, he visits the oldest eel and pie shop in London. S1, Ep3
Rick experiences the Lake District's quality produce. He cooks a Chinese-inspired wild boar stew. Plus, he meets a farmer who keeps Herdwick sheep. S1, Ep4
